currentAffairs.headline: Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill Defeated in Lok Sabha
currentAffairs.detail: The Bill aimed to decouple women's 33% reservation (106th Amendment) from a post-2026 Census by allowing use of 2011 Census data for delimitation (amending Article 82), and proposed expanding Lok Sabha from 550 to 850 seats. It received 298 votes vs the required 352 two-thirds majority. Southern states opposed seat expansion due to demographic disadvantage.
currentAffairs.tags: Women Reservation, 131st Amendment, Delimitation, Article 82, 106th Amendment, Lok Sabha seats
currentAffairs.mcq: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill 2026 proposed amending which Article to enable delimitation based on 2011 Census? → Article 82

currentAffairs.headline: India Hosts 16th BRICS NSA Meeting in New Delhi (June 22–23)
currentAffairs.detail: India, as BRICS Chair 2026, hosted the National Security Advisers' conclave chaired by NSA Ajit Doval. Theme: 'Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today'. Delegates included China's Wang Yi and Russia's NSA Sergei Shoigu. Discussions covered cyber threats, AI-driven risks, counter-terrorism. The meet prepares ground for the BRICS Summit in September 2026 in India.
currentAffairs.tags: BRICS 2026, BRICS Chair India, NSA Meeting, Ajit Doval, Non-traditional security, Cyber threats
currentAffairs.mcq: India is BRICS Chair in 2026. The theme of the BRICS NSA Meeting held in June 2026 was? → Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today

currentAffairs.headline: India's FY2026 GDP at 7.7%; RBI Keeps Repo Rate at 5.25%
currentAffairs.detail: India's real GDP grew 7.7% in FY2026 (revised up from 7.6%), making it the fastest-growing G20 economy. RBI's MPC held the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% (SDF: 5.00%; MSF: 5.50%). Forex reserves stand at $682.3 billion (~11 months import cover). FY2027 GDP projected at 6.6%. CPI inflation projected at 5.1% for FY2027; crude oil at ~$110/barrel poses upside risk.
currentAffairs.tags: GDP 7.7%, Repo Rate 5.25%, Forex Reserves $682B, RBI MPC, CPI Inflation, India Economy
currentAffairs.mcq: India's Forex reserves as of May 29, 2026 stood at? → $682.3 billion (approximately 11 months import cover)

currentAffairs.headline: India-UAE in Talks for ~$4 Billion BrahMos & Akashteer Deal
currentAffairs.detail: UAE is in early talks to buy BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer (fully automated air defence C2 system by BEL/Indian Army) from India — a potential $4 billion deal. India's defence exports hit ₹33,000+ crore in FY2026 (from ₹60 crore in 2013-14). BrahMos debuted in combat during the 2025 India-Pakistan conflict; deals with Vietnam and Indonesia already signed.
currentAffairs.tags: BrahMos, Akashteer, BEL, Defence Exports, UAE, India-Pakistan 2025, Operation Sindoor, ₹33000 crore
currentAffairs.mcq: Akashteer, recently in news for export deals, is developed by which Indian entity? → Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) in partnership with Indian Army

currentAffairs.headline: Western Ghats ESA Notification Imminent After 12-Year Delay
currentAffairs.detail: The Central Government is finalising the Ecologically Sensitive Area (ESA) notification for the Western Ghats, 12 years after the first draft. The Ghats are one of 8 global biodiversity hotspots and a UNESCO World Heritage Site; home to 30% of world's Asian elephants and 17% of tigers. ~245 million people depend on its rivers (Godavari, Krishna, Cauvery). ESA notification regulates mining, quarrying, construction.
currentAffairs.tags: Western Ghats, ESA, Eco-Sensitive Zone, Kasturirangan, Biodiversity Hotspot, UNESCO WHS, Nilgiri tahr
currentAffairs.mcq: The Kasturirangan Committee (2013) was associated with which environmental issue? → Western Ghats Ecologically Sensitive Area (ESA) boundary delineation

currentAffairs.headline: ISRO & Jal Shakti Sign MoU for Satellite-Based Water Management
currentAffairs.detail: ISRO and the Ministry of Jal Shakti are signing an MoU to use satellite remote sensing for water resource management across 24 priority areas. This supports Jal Jeevan Mission (tap water to rural households) and Atal Bhujal Yojana (groundwater management). Separately, Aditya-L1 (India's solar observatory) has released 27 TB of solar observation data to the global public domain.
currentAffairs.tags: ISRO, Jal Shakti, MoU, Water Management, Remote Sensing, Jal Jeevan Mission, Aditya-L1
currentAffairs.mcq: Which ministry collaborated with ISRO for satellite-based water management across 24 priority areas in June 2026? → Ministry of Jal Shakti

currentAffairs.headline: TRISHNA: ISRO–France (CNES) Joint Thermal Imaging Satellite Launched
currentAffairs.detail: TRISHNA (Thermal infraRed Imaging Satellite for High-resolution Natural resource Assessment) is a joint 2026 mission of ISRO and CNES (France). It monitors crop water stress and urban heat islands using high-resolution thermal infrared imagery — critical for food security planning and climate adaptation. Earlier ISRO-CNES collaboration includes Megha-Tropiques (2011).
currentAffairs.tags: TRISHNA, ISRO, CNES, France, Thermal Infrared, Crop Water Stress, Urban Heat Island, Megha-Tropiques
currentAffairs.mcq: TRISHNA, a joint satellite mission of ISRO and which country's space agency, monitors crop water stress and urban heat islands? → France (CNES — Centre National d'Études Spatiales)

currentAffairs.headline: EAM Jaishankar Visits Mongolia; Focus on Energy & Connectivity
currentAffairs.detail: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar visited Mongolia on June 22–23, meeting its leadership for talks on energy, infrastructure, and strategic connectivity. Mongolia is landlocked between Russia and China with uranium and coal reserves — significant for India's energy security and Act East Policy. India follows a 'Third Neighbour Policy' with Mongolia.
currentAffairs.tags: Mongolia, Jaishankar, Act East Policy, Third Neighbour Policy, Extended Neighbourhood, Energy Security, Uranium
currentAffairs.mcq: India's 'Third Neighbour Policy' is specifically associated with which country? → Mongolia

currentAffairs.headline: G7 Summit 2026 at Évian, France — Ukraine, Trade, AI Agenda
currentAffairs.detail: The G7 Summit (June 15-17, Évian-les-Bains, France) reaffirmed support for Ukraine's sovereignty and welcomed the Global Convergence for Growth Summit (with China). Divergences persisted on US tariffs, NATO commitments, Middle East stance, and AI governance. The G7 'managed rather than resolved' differences. France is the 2026 G7 Chair.
currentAffairs.tags: G7 2026, Évian, France, G7 Chair France, Ukraine, NATO, AI Governance, US Tariffs, Global Imbalances
currentAffairs.mcq: The G7 Summit 2026 was hosted at which location in France? → Évian-les-Bains

currentAffairs.headline: IMF Projects 3.1% Global Growth for 2026; World Bank Cuts to 2.5%
currentAffairs.detail: IMF's WEO April 2026 (titled 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War') projects global growth at 3.1% for 2026, with global headline inflation at 4.4%. World Bank cut its 2026 forecast to 2.5% (risk scenario: 1.3%) due to the Middle East war and energy shocks (~19% crude price rise). EMDE growth at post-pandemic low of 3.6%; MENA+Pakistan region cut by 2.7pp to 1.6%.
currentAffairs.tags: IMF, World Bank, Global Growth 2026, Middle East War, EMDE, Strait of Hormuz, WEO, Energy Shock
currentAffairs.mcq: The IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook was titled? → 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War' — projected 3.1% global growth for 2026

currentAffairs.headline: Ashtalakshmi Growth Model: NE India's 8 States as Growth Engines
currentAffairs.detail: The 'Ashtalakshmi' (Eight Lakshmi) model coined by PM Modi refers to India's 8 NE states (Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim) being positioned as growth engines via infrastructure, Act East connectivity, and hydropower. The model is gaining international attention as a framework for integrating border regions into national development.
currentAffairs.tags: Ashtalakshmi, North East India, 8 NE States, Act East Policy, LPAI, Kaladan Multi-Modal, PM Modi
currentAffairs.mcq: The 'Ashtalakshmi' model refers to the development of how many North-Eastern states? → 8 (Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim)

currentAffairs.title: INS Dunagiri, INS Agray & INS Sanshodhak Commissioned
currentAffairs.summary: PM Modi commissioned three indigenously built naval vessels at Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port, Kolkata. INS Dunagiri is a stealth frigate, INS Agray is an anti-submarine warfare ship, and INS Sanshodhak is an advanced hydrographic survey vessel.

currentAffairs.title: GRSE Conferred Navratna Status
currentAffairs.summary: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd (GRSE) was officially granted Navratna status by the Government of India on 19 June 2026, making it one of India's premier defence PSUs.

currentAffairs.title: PM-KISAN 23rd Instalment Released — ₹18,880 Crore
currentAffairs.summary: PM Modi released the 23rd instalment of PM Kisan Samman Nidhi on 20 June 2026, transferring ₹18,880 crore directly to farmers' bank accounts under the ₹6,000/year direct benefit scheme.

currentAffairs.title: PMFBY Launched in West Bengal
currentAffairs.summary: PM Modi launched the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in West Bengal during his 20-21 June visit, extending the world's largest crop insurance scheme to farmers in the state.

currentAffairs.title: 12th International Day of Yoga Observed at Kolkata
currentAffairs.summary: PM Modi led the national observance of the 12th International Day of Yoga from Red Road in Kolkata on 21 June 2026. The UN declared June 21 as International Yoga Day in 2014.

currentAffairs.title: FCRA Rules 2011 Amended via Gazette Notification
currentAffairs.summary: The Union Home Ministry amended the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Rules, 2011 through a gazette notification on 22 June 2026, tightening norms for foreign funding to NGOs and organisations.

currentAffairs.title: NMC Discontinues Postgraduate Diploma Medical Courses
currentAffairs.summary: The National Medical Commission decided to discontinue PG diploma courses and shift specialist medical education fully towards MD and MS degree programmes to standardise medical education quality.

currentAffairs.title: India to Transfer PSLV Technology to Private Sector
currentAffairs.summary: India plans to transfer Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) technology to private companies through IN-SPACe, aiming to boost private space sector participation and reduce ISRO's manufacturing burden.

currentAffairs.title: CURAJ Develops Sensor to Detect Lead in Drinking Water
currentAffairs.summary: Researchers at Central University of Rajasthan (CURAJ) developed a sensor on 22 June 2026 capable of detecting minute amounts of toxic lead ions in drinking water, a breakthrough for water safety monitoring.

currentAffairs.title: IAF Issues RFP for Rafale Bridge Support Package
currentAffairs.summary: The Indian Air Force issued a Request for Proposal in June 2026 for a five-month bridge support package for all 36 Rafale fighter aircraft covering maintenance, logistics and technical support.

currentAffairs.title: India Remains ADB's Largest Private Sector Market
currentAffairs.summary: India remains the largest market for the Asian Development Bank's private sector operations, reflecting India's growing economic significance and attractiveness for multilateral investment.

currentAffairs.title: NSE Signs MoU with Bharat Metal Exchange for Non-Ferrous Metal Derivatives
currentAffairs.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Bharat Metal Exchange (BME), formerly Bombay Metal Exchange Limited, to promote non-ferrous metal derivatives in India. The partnership strengthens price risk management and enhances the derivatives ecosystem for metals like copper, aluminium and zinc. This collaboration aims to benefit traders, hedgers and industrial users across the supply chain.

currentAffairs.title: India Wins Silver in Mixed 4x400m Relay, Bronze in Mixed 4x100m Relay
currentAffairs.summary: India secured silver in the mixed 4x400m relay through the quartet of MR Poovamma, Neeru Pathak, Theerthesh P Shetty, and Barath Sridhar. India also won bronze in the mixed 4x100m relay through Animesh Kujur, Pranav Gurav, Tamanna, and Sneha Shanuvalli with a time of 41.47 seconds. The medals highlight India's growing strength in track and field relay events.

currentAffairs.title: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla Launches Book on IIT at IIT Delhi
currentAffairs.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla launched the book titled 'IIT: The Story of India's Most Prestigious Educational Ecosystem' authored by Prabhat Kumar, an IRS officer and Chairman of PanIIT Alumni India, at a special event held at IIT Delhi. The book chronicles the journey of the Indian Institutes of Technology from their inception to becoming globally renowned centres of excellence. The launch underscores the significance of IITs in India's technological and economic development.

currentAffairs.title: India's MRFA Program to Procure 114 Additional Rafale Jets from Dassault
currentAffairs.summary: India is pressing ahead with its Multi-Role Fighter Aircraft (MRFA) program to procure 114 additional Rafale jets from France's Dassault Aviation. Under the program, 18 Rafales will be delivered in flyaway condition, while the remaining aircraft will be manufactured in India with approximately 50 per cent indigenous content. The programme aims to significantly boost the Indian Air Force's combat capabilities while advancing the 'Make in India' defence initiative.

currentAffairs.title: EAM Jaishankar Meets Mongolia's Foreign Minister; Focus on Economic Cooperation
currentAffairs.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held discussions with Mongolia's Foreign Minister focused on transforming cordial bilateral ties into tangible economic outcomes. Key areas of cooperation identified include mining, clean energy, agricultural processing, education, security, and multilateral forums. The discussions aim to deepen the India-Mongolia strategic partnership beyond diplomatic engagement.

currentAffairs.title: Indian Astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla Onboard Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04)
currentAffairs.summary: ISRO successfully launched Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04) with Indian astronaut pilot Shubhanshu Shukla onboard SpaceX's Dragon Crew Module to the International Space Station. The mission marks a landmark achievement for India's human spaceflight programme, paving the way for the Gaganyaan mission. Shukla becomes only the second Indian to visit the International Space Station.

currentAffairs.title: India's Bioeconomy Crosses $190 Billion — 20-Fold Growth Since 2014
currentAffairs.summary: India's bioeconomy has undergone an unprecedented twenty-fold expansion, growing from nearly $10 billion in 2014 to over $190 billion in 2026. This growth spans biopharmaceuticals, agricultural biotechnology, industrial biotech, and biofuels. India's Union Budget has bet heavily on biopharma and space to sustain this momentum ahead of upcoming human spaceflight missions.

currentAffairs.title: UP CM Yogi Sets June 2026 Deadline for Three Expressway Land Acquisitions
currentAffairs.summary: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to complete land acquisition for the Agra-Lucknow-Purvanchal Link Expressway, Jewar Link Expressway, and Jhansi Link Expressway by end of June 2026. The Chief Minister also emphasised rapid expansion of electric vehicle charging infrastructure in the state. These expressways are part of UP's ₹66,000 crore infrastructure push under Budget 2026.

currentAffairs.title: Uttar Pradesh Targets 10,000 EV Charging Stations by 2030; 15.5 Lakh EVs Registered
currentAffairs.summary: Uttar Pradesh currently has nearly 15.5 lakh registered electric vehicles, and the state government aims to establish 10,000 EV charging stations by 2030. The state has emerged as one of India's largest EV markets, driven by policy incentives and rising consumer adoption. Expansion of charging infrastructure is a key priority under UP's clean energy and urban mobility agenda.

currentAffairs.title: IMD Forecasts Monsoon to Cover More Parts of Maharashtra, Telangana & Eastern India
currentAffairs.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast that the southwest monsoon is likely to cover more parts of Maharashtra, Telangana, and eastern India with heavy rain and thunderstorms forecast across regions till June 28. The timely monsoon advancement is crucial for Kharif crop sowing, which typically begins in June-July across India. Normal or above-normal rainfall in key agricultural states is expected to positively impact food production.

uppscNews.headline: Ganga Expressway Inauguration Imminent; 600 km Across 12 Districts
uppscNews.detail: The Ganga Expressway (600 km, 12 districts) is largely complete and expected to be inaugurated in 2026. Nine wayside amenities are being developed along the route including EV charging, motels, and fuel stations. UP has allocated ₹66,000 crore for roads, bridges, and expressways in Budget 2026-27.
uppscNews.tags: Ganga Expressway, UPEIDA, UP Budget 2026, Expressway, Infrastructure
uppscNews.mcq: The Ganga Expressway in UP spans across how many districts? → 12 districts (600 km length)

uppscNews.headline: Yogi Govt Approves Cashless Medical Treatment for 15 Lakh Education Dept Staff
uppscNews.detail: The UP government formally approved a cashless medical treatment scheme for ~15 lakh teachers and non-teaching staff (and their families) under the Basic and Secondary Education Departments. This is part of the Yogi government's welfare push for government employees in the education sector.
uppscNews.tags: UP Schemes, Cashless Medical, Education Department, Yogi Government, Teachers
uppscNews.mcq: The UP government's cashless medical treatment scheme covers employees of which two departments? → Basic Education and Secondary Education Departments

uppscNews.headline: UP Launches Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana for Indigenous Cows
uppscNews.detail: The Yogi government launched the Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana to increase high-quality indigenous cow population, promote dairy farming, and create employment for youth and women in rural areas. The scheme focuses on improving indigenous (desi) cow breeds over exotic breeds.
uppscNews.tags: Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana, Indigenous Cows, Dairy Farming, UP Agriculture, Rural Employment
uppscNews.mcq: Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana launched in UP aims to promote? → Indigenous (desi) cow breeds, dairy farming, and rural employment

uppscNews.headline: SC Stays Allahabad HC Order on ₹10 Lakh Compensation for Illegal UP Police Detention
uppscNews.detail: The Supreme Court stayed an Allahabad High Court order directing the UP government to pay ₹10 lakh compensation to a man allegedly held in illegal police detention for 3+ months. The HC had held the arrest illegal under Article 22(1) — failure to provide written grounds of arrest. The SC bench of Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and Sanjeev Sachdeva passed the interim stay.
uppscNews.tags: Article 22, Illegal Detention, UP Police, Supreme Court, Allahabad HC, Fundamental Rights
uppscNews.mcq: Article 22(1) of the Constitution mandates that an arrested person must be? → Informed of the grounds of arrest and allowed to consult a legal practitioner of their choice

uppscNews.headline: BRICS Culture Working Group Meeting Held in Varanasi, UP
uppscNews.detail: India hosted the 2nd BRICS Culture Working Group (CWG) Meeting in Varanasi — one of the world's oldest living cities and UP's cultural capital. The meeting focused on cultural heritage conservation, creative industries, museum cooperation, and cultural tourism among BRICS nations. Varanasi's selection reinforces its significance as India's soft power hub.
uppscNews.tags: BRICS 2026, Varanasi, Culture Working Group, Cultural Heritage, Soft Power, UP Tourism
uppscNews.mcq: The 2nd BRICS Culture Working Group Meeting in June 2026 was held in which UP city? → Varanasi

uppscNews.headline: UP's Groundwater Crisis Deepens; 500 STPs Treating Only 25% of Wastewater
uppscNews.detail: A CSE analysis highlights UP's worsening groundwater crisis driven by water-intensive crops (wheat, sugarcane) and over-extraction via tube wells. UP has 500 STPs with 2,000 MLD capacity but treats only ~500 MLD. Ganga pollution continues due to 1.5 million tons of annual crop residue burning and industrial effluents from small units along the river.
uppscNews.tags: Groundwater, Ganga Pollution, UP STP, Namami Gange, Crop Residue Burning, Water Crisis
uppscNews.mcq: UP has 500 STPs with treatment capacity of 2000 MLD, but actual treatment is only? → ~500 MLD (25% utilisation)

uppscNews.headline: UPPSC PCS Prelims 2026 Scheduled for December 6; UP Special Paper Covers 20-30% Syllabus
uppscNews.detail: UPPSC PCS Prelims 2026 is scheduled for 6 December 2026. GS Papers V & VI are UP-specific, covering UP history, art, architecture, culture, geography, governance, economy, and demography. UP-specific content forms 20-30% of total UPPSC PCS questions. Aspirants must focus on state schemes, geography (rivers, soil), culture, and local governance.
uppscNews.tags: UPPSC PCS 2026, Prelims Date, UP Special Paper, PCS Syllabus, December 6
uppscNews.mcq: UPPSC PCS Prelims 2026 is scheduled on? → 6 December 2026

uppscNews.headline: UP Cabinet Raises Wheat MSP by ₹160/Quintal; Additional ₹20 for Handling
uppscNews.detail: The UP cabinet chaired by CM Yogi Adityanath approved an increase of ₹160/quintal in wheat MSP for the current procurement season, along with ₹20/quintal extra for cleaning, sorting and handling costs to benefit farmers directly.

uppscNews.headline: Oracle Partners with UPSDM to Train 3 Lakh Youth in AI, Cloud & Cybersecurity by 2029
uppscNews.detail: Oracle announced a partnership with UP State Skill Development Mission (UPSDM) to deliver skills training in AI, cloud computing, cybersecurity and data science to 3 lakh students and professionals across UP by 2029.

uppscNews.headline: UP Governor Mandates Uniforms for All Universities and Colleges in State
uppscNews.detail: UP Governor Anandiben Patel directed all universities and colleges in Uttar Pradesh to implement a uniform dress code for students, issued at a meeting in Jan Bhavan to promote discipline and equality in higher education.

uppscNews.headline: UP CM Yogi Sets June Deadline for Three Expressway Land Acquisitions
uppscNews.detail: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to complete land acquisition for the Agra-Lucknow-Purvanchal Link, Jewar Link, and Jhansi Link Expressways by end of June 2026. These projects are part of the state's ₹66,000 crore infrastructure budget allocation. The Agra-Lucknow-Purvanchal Link Expressway will be a key connectivity corridor across the state.

uppscNews.headline: UP Targets 10,000 EV Charging Stations by 2030; State Has 15.5 Lakh Registered EVs
uppscNews.detail: Uttar Pradesh has 15.5 lakh registered electric vehicles and the government has set a target of 10,000 EV charging stations by 2030. The state also hosts data centres with a 700 MW capacity (under development or operational) worth over ₹22,000 crore, positioning UP as a digital and green energy hub. These initiatives align with the CM's vision of a 'Developed Uttar Pradesh'.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Economy
dailyQuizQuestions.question: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) signed a MoU with Bharat Metal Exchange (BME) to promote which category of financial instruments in India?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: NSE signed a MoU with BME (formerly Bombay Metal Exchange Limited) specifically to promote the development and adoption of non-ferrous metal derivatives such as copper, aluminium and zinc. This partnership strengthens price risk management for industrial users and traders in these metals.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Defence
dailyQuizQuestions.question: Under India's Multi-Role Fighter Aircraft (MRFA) programme, how many additional Rafale jets is India planning to procure from Dassault Aviation?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: India's MRFA programme aims to procure 114 additional Rafale jets from France's Dassault Aviation, supplementing the original 36 Rafale order. Of these, 18 will be delivered in flyaway condition while the rest will be manufactured in India with approximately 50% indigenous content.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Defence
dailyQuizQuestions.question: The Indian Air Force issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) for a bridge support package for Rafale aircraft in June 2026. For how many months is this bridge support package?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The IAF issued an RFP for a five-month bridge support package covering all 36 Rafale fighter aircraft, including maintenance, logistics and technical support. This is intended to ensure uninterrupted operations until a long-term support contract is finalised.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Economy
dailyQuizQuestions.question: India's bioeconomy has crossed $190 billion in 2026. Approximately what was its value in 2014, representing a nearly twenty-fold growth?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: India's bioeconomy has expanded from nearly $10 billion in 2014 to over $190 billion in 2026, representing a twenty-fold growth. This rapid expansion covers biopharmaceuticals, agricultural biotech, industrial biotech, and biofuels, making India a global leader in the bioeconomy sector.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Agriculture
dailyQuizQuestions.question: IMD forecast in June 2026 that the southwest monsoon would cover more parts of Maharashtra, Telangana and eastern India with heavy rains till what date?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast that heavy rain and thunderstorms would continue across Maharashtra, Telangana, and eastern India till June 28, 2026. This is significant for Kharif crop sowing as timely monsoon advancement directly impacts food production in these agricultural states.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Polity
dailyQuizQuestions.question: When EAM S. Jaishankar met Mongolia's Foreign Minister in June 2026, which of the following was NOT mentioned as a key area of bilateral cooperation?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The Jaishankar-Mongolia talks focused on mining, clean energy, agricultural processing, education, security, and cooperation in multilateral forums. Defence technology transfer was not among the areas specifically highlighted. The meeting aimed to transform cordial ties into tangible economic outcomes.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Science & Technology
dailyQuizQuestions.question: Which Indian astronaut pilot flew onboard SpaceX's Dragon Crew Module as part of Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04) to the International Space Station?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: Indian astronaut pilot Shubhanshu Shukla flew onboard SpaceX's Dragon Crew Module as part of Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04) to the International Space Station. He becomes only the second Indian to visit the ISS, and the mission represents a major step towards ISRO's human spaceflight Gaganyaan programme.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Science & Technology
dailyQuizQuestions.question: How many foreign satellites had India launched from 2014 up to March 2026, demonstrating India's growing role in commercial space activities?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: After 2014 and up to March 2026, India has launched 399 foreign satellites from various countries, showcasing ISRO's growing commercial launch capabilities. This positions India as a cost-effective and reliable partner in global commercial space activities.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: National
dailyQuizQuestions.question: In which relay event did India win the silver medal through the quartet of MR Poovamma, Neeru Pathak, Theerthesh P Shetty, and Barath Sridhar?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: India secured the silver medal in the mixed 4x400m relay through MR Poovamma, Neeru Pathak, Theerthesh P Shetty, and Barath Sridhar. India also won a bronze in the mixed 4x100m relay through a different team clocking 41.47 seconds, demonstrating India's growing strength in track relay events.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: UP Special
dailyQuizQuestions.question: How many EV charging stations is Uttar Pradesh targeting to establish by 2030?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The Uttar Pradesh government aims to establish 10,000 EV charging stations by 2030. The state already has approximately 15.5 lakh registered electric vehicles, making it one of India's largest EV markets. This initiative is part of UP's clean energy and sustainable urban mobility agenda.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: UP Special
dailyQuizQuestions.question: UP CM Yogi Adityanath set a June 2026 deadline for land acquisition for three expressways. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The three expressways for which CM Yogi set a June 2026 land acquisition deadline are the Agra-Lucknow-Purvanchal Link Expressway, Jewar Link Expressway, and Jhansi Link Expressway. The Gorakhpur Link Expressway was not part of this specific deadline directive in June 2026.

dailyQuizQuestions.subject: Health
dailyQuizQuestions.question: In June 2026, the Union Government announced plans for a second campus of which premier mental health institute to expand mental healthcare infrastructure in India?
dailyQuizQuestions.optD: LGB Regional Institute of Mental Health, Tezpur
dailyQuizQuestions.explanation: The Union Government announced plans for a second National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro Sciences (NIMHANS) to expand mental health infrastructure across India. The government also proposed upgrades to existing psychiatric centres in Ranchi and Tezpur. NIMHANS in Bengaluru is India's apex centre for mental health research, education and patient care.
